findings.title: Any allowlisted address can mint the entire public allocation in a single free claim

best_agent_match.title: Any allowlisted address can mint the entire public allocation in a single free claim

findings.title: Owner can inflate supply beyond `maxsupply` by resetting `reserve` and minting again

best_agent_match.title: Owner can inflate supply beyond `maxsupply` by resetting `reserve` and minting again

findings.title: Reentrant `_safeMint` can reuse a stale `_currentIndex` and corrupt ERC721A accounting

best_agent_match.title: Reentrancy in `_safeMint` uses a stale `_currentIndex` and can corrupt ownership/accounting

findings.title: Owner can arbitrarily change metadata and reversibly hide revealed NFTs

best_agent_match.title: Owner can arbitrarily rug metadata by changing URIs and toggling reveal state

rejected_candidates.title: Owner Can Add Themselves to Whitelist After Deployment
rejected_candidates.reason: `setRootHash` is a privileged allowlist-management function. By itself it does not bypass the supply checks or create an independent over-mint path; the meaningful supply-breaking issue is already captured by the mutable `reserve` finding.

rejected_candidates.title: Withdraw Function Will Fail Due to Incorrect Syntax
rejected_candidates.reason: `payable(msg.sender).transfer(balance)` is valid Solidity 0.8.x syntax, so this is not a real compile-time or runtime bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Zero Quantity Mint Check Commented Out
rejected_candidates.reason: The only reachable zero-quantity path is the owner-only `mintReservedTokens(0)`, which does not mint tokens and does not create realistic protocol-level harm.
